title: Shorea maranti
intro: Shorea maranti, also known as Maranti, is a tropical tree species native to Malaysia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Shorea maranti include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Shorea maranti is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used to make capsules and tinctures for treating various health conditions.
taxonomy: Shorea maranti is classified under the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the dipterocarps. This family is native to tropical Asia and consists of over 500 species. Shorea maranti is specifically classified under the genus Shorea, which is a large genus of flowering plants in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The genus Shorea is characterized by its large trees with broad leaves and showy flowers. Shorea maranti is an important species in its ecosystem, providing food and shelter for various animals.
uses: Shorea maranti is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, and research has identified its potential medicinal uses. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have applications in wound healing, as well as in the treatment of various infections. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Shorea maranti's medicinal uses. Traditional healers in the region have been using the plant for centuries to treat various ailments, further highlighting its potential as a medicinal resource.
benefits: Shorea maranti, a type of tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, has been studied for its potential health benefits. The extracts from its bark and leaves have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. Additionally, Shorea maranti has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. These include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. parts: Shorea maranti, also known as the meranti, is a species of tree native to Southeast Asia. The bark is used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, while the leaves are used to treat respiratory issues and the resin is used to treat skin infections. preparations: Shorea maranti is also known as the "Benguet" or "Sarangan" tree, and its bark and roots are used in traditional herbal preparations.
